name: EJDB2
description: EJDB2 is an embeddable JSON database engine written in C, published under the MIT license. It stores BSON documents in a single file backed by the IOWOW key/value storage engine. It features a powerful XPath-inspired query language (JQL) with support for collection joins, projections, sorting, and data modification. EJDB2 also includes an optional HTTP/WebSocket REST endpoint for network-accessible deployments. Language bindings exist for C, Go, Node.js, and Python.
format_on_disk: Single file (IOWOW key/value storage engine with BSON documents)
core_strengths: XPath-inspired JQL query language with joins, projections, and sorting
Single-file embedded database with zero configuration required
Online backups support without stopping the database
Optional HTTP/WebSocket REST endpoint for network access
ACID-compliant with indexes on JSON fields for fast queries
Compact C11 implementation suitable for IoT and edge devices
best_use_cases: Embedded JSON storage for desktop and CLI applications
IoT and edge device data persistence with minimal footprint
Mobile app local storage via Swift/Kotlin bindings
Lightweight document store for microservices and prototypes
Applications needing JSON-native querying with join capabilities
remarks: The C library must be compiled from source. Python bindings are community-maintained via ctypes/CFFI (no official pip package). Node.js bindings require native compilation. The JQL query language uses a custom syntax distinct from SQL or MongoDB — there is a learning curve. Concurrent write access is serialized through a file lock.
